items.title: National Workshop on Social Justice Concludes in Chandigarh
items.whyInNews: The national workshop on social justice concluded in Chandigarh with a focus on achieving an Antyodaya-inspired developed India by 2047. The workshop was attended by various stakeholders and experts in the field of social justice.
items.summary: The national workshop on social justice was held in Chandigarh from April 22-24, 2026. The workshop aimed to achieve an Antyodaya-inspired developed India by 2047. The stakeholders discussed various issues related to social justice and formulated a roadmap to achieve the goal.

The workshop aimed to achieve an Antyodaya-inspired developed India by 2047

A roadmap was formulated to achieve the goal

The government has launched various schemes to promote social justice, including the Pradhan Mantri Jan Dhan Yojana
The workshop is a step towards achieving the United Nations' Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs)

items.title: India to Achieve Technical Sovereignty
items.whyInNews: Dr. Jitendra Singh, Minister of State for Science and Technology, stated that India needs to lead in important technologies to achieve technical sovereignty.
items.summary: Dr. Jitendra Singh, Minister of State for Science and Technology, emphasized the need for India to lead in important technologies to achieve technical sovereignty. He highlighted the importance of self-reliance in technology and innovation. The government has launched various initiatives to promote indigenous technology and reduce dependence on foreign technologies.
keyPoints: India needs to lead in important technologies to achieve technical sovereignty
Dr. Jitendra Singh emphasized the need for self-reliance in technology and innovation
The government has launched various initiatives to promote indigenous technology
The goal is to reduce dependence on foreign technologies

The Constitution of India, under Article 51, promotes international peace and security
The government has launched initiatives such as the Make in India and Start-up India programs
India has made significant progress in areas such as space technology and nuclear energy
Technical sovereignty is essential for India's economic and strategic growth

items.title: NITI Aayog Releases Report on Effective Urban Governance
items.whyInNews: NITI Aayog released a report on effective urban governance, focusing on cities with a population of over 1 million.
items.summary: NITI Aayog released a report on effective urban governance, titled 'Moving towards Effective Urban Governance – A Framework for Cities with a Population of over 1 Million'. The report highlights the challenges faced by Indian cities and provides a framework for effective urban governance. The report emphasizes the need for a holistic approach to urban planning and development.
keyPoints: NITI Aayog released a report on effective urban governance
The report focuses on cities with a population of over 1 million

A framework is provided for effective urban governance
importantPoints: NITI Aayog was established in 2015 and is headquartered in New Delhi
The 74th Constitutional Amendment Act, 1992, gives constitutional status to urban local bodies
The report is a step towards achieving the United Nations' Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs)
The government has launched various initiatives to promote urban development, including the Smart Cities Mission
Effective urban governance is essential for India's economic and social growth

items.title: Prime Minister to Inaugurate Ganga Expressway
items.whyInNews: Prime Minister Narendra Modi will inaugurate the Ganga Expressway in Uttar Pradesh on April 29, 2026.
items.summary: Prime Minister Narendra Modi will inaugurate the Ganga Expressway in Uttar Pradesh on April 29, 2026. The expressway is the longest in Uttar Pradesh and will connect the cities of Prayagraj and Meerut. The project is expected to boost economic growth and development in the region.
keyPoints: Prime Minister Narendra Modi will inaugurate the Ganga Expressway

The project is a step towards achieving the government's goal of developing world-class infrastructure

items.title: Vaibhav Suryavanshi Becomes Youngest to Score 1,000 Runs in T20s
items.whyInNews: Vaibhav Suryavanshi achieved this feat in the Indian Premier League (IPL) 2026, having faced just 473 balls in the format. He hit 12 sixes and five boundaries as he completed his second century in the IPL.
items.summary: Vaibhav Suryavanshi has become the youngest player to score 1,000 runs in T20 cricket, achieving this feat in the Indian Premier League (IPL) 2026. He reached this milestone while playing for his team in the IPL, with a strike rate of over 200. Suryavanshi's achievement is a significant one, as he has faced just 473 balls in the format to reach 1,000 runs.

items.title: KL Rahul Becomes First Indian to Cross the 150-Run Mark in an Innings
items.whyInNews: KL Rahul achieved this feat in the Indian Premier League (IPL) 2026, bringing up his century in just 47 balls. He outdid Virender Sehwag and Quinton de Kock's 48-ball centuries in 2011 and 2016, respectively.
items.summary: KL Rahul has become the first Indian player to cross the 150-run mark in an innings in the Indian Premier League (IPL) 2026. items.title: Kenya's Sawe Becomes First Man to Run Marathon Under Two Hours
items.whyInNews: Eliud Sawe achieved this feat at the London Marathon, shattering the world record previously held by Kelvin Kiptum. Sawe completed the marathon in under two hours, with a time of 1:59:59.
items.summary: Eliud Sawe has become the first man to run a marathon under two hours, achieving this feat at the London Marathon. He shattered the world record previously held by Kelvin Kiptum, completing the marathon in under two hours. Sawe's achievement is a significant one, as he has pushed the boundaries of human endurance in long-distance running.
